subject: Chemistry
title: Alizarin: Replacing Madder Root
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Alizarin: Replacing Madder Root.

hook.text: For 3,000 years, red dye came from madder root. In 1868, two chemists synthesized alizarin and wiped out the industry.

buildup.text: Madder root contains alizarin, a molecule that binds to fabric through metal ions called mordants.

discovery.text: Graebe and Liebermann figured out alizarin's structure and synthesized it from coal tar at a fraction of the cost.

twist.text: Madder root farming collapsed across Europe. Thousands of acres were abandoned within a decade of the synthesis.

climax.text: British soldiers' red coats had been dyed with madder. Synthetic alizarin made the famous 'redcoats' even cheaper.

punchline.text: Same molecule, different source. Synthesis replaced a 3,000-year-old harvest.

quiz.question: What happened when alizarin was synthesized artificially?
options: Madder root farming collapsed across Europe within a decade
The synthetic version was a different color than natural alizarin
Governments banned synthetic dyes to protect farmers
